asset_alt: Archived embodied music pipeline from robot actions to MIDI events and piano performance
asset_caption: Archive diagram: action/contact-to-MIDI and real robot piano performance are retained as references, not an active survey project.
title: Archive: EmbodiedMusic
description: Archive reason: HandelBot now covers the main real-world embodied-music target: precise bimanual robot piano playing through sim-trained dexterous policies, rapid real-world refinement, residual RL, and MIDI-based rewards.
summary: EmbodiedMusic is archived as a standalone Survey project. HandelBot demonstrates the exact measurable loop this card was trying to formulate: robot action policies grounded in a real instrument, MIDI/event feedback as the objective, and hardware experiments across recognized songs. Remaining music/audio-generation ideas should only reopen if they move beyond piano performance into a distinct contact-to-sound or multimodal instrument dataset that is tied to current lab hardware.

details.text: HandelBot completes the actionable piano-performance version of this topic: it starts from a simulation-trained policy, applies structured real-world refinement to correct finger/key alignment, then uses residual RL with keyboard MIDI output as reward. The system performs precise bimanual piano playing on real hardware across five recognized songs and reports a 1.8x improvement over direct simulation deployment with about 30 minutes of physical interaction data.

Earlier references such as RoboPianist and FürElise remain useful background for simulation and humanoid piano baselines, but HandelBot closes the missing real-world adaptation/evaluation gap that kept this card in Survey.

details: Moved-out live work: if the lab later wants music again, it should be a narrower dataset or hardware card, such as tactile/contact-to-sound for a non-piano instrument, motion-to-rhythm from human body data, or evaluation tooling that directly supports an active robot manipulation project.

Archive decision: do not keep a general EmbodiedMusic survey unless there is a distinct instrument, sensor stack, dataset owner, and metric that is not already addressed by real-world MIDI-supervised piano performance.
risks_decisions: Archived 2026-06-05 after user flagged HandelBot as completing the desired effect.
Reason type: paper/project completed the core measurable embodied-music loop for robot action -> instrument event -> real-world policy adaptation.
Generic audio-generation or body-rhythm ideas are not enough to reopen this card unless they become a concrete robotics dataset or demo.
Reopen condition: a named owner commits to a non-piano embodied instrument task with synchronized action/contact/audio data and metrics beyond MIDI event correctness.
